name: Gas And Electric
description_short: Gas & Electric Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution in Rock Island, IL offering checking, savings, certificates, auto loans, and home loans with competitive rates.
description_long:
Gas & Electric Credit Union (GECU) is a credit union based in Rock Island, Illinois, serving members through a community-focused financial services model. As a credit union, it operates as a member-owned cooperative rather than a shareholder-owned bank, which typically allows it to offer competitive rates and personalized service. The organization emphasizes member relationships and financial wellness, positioning itself as a family-oriented institution that prioritizes member support.

GECU offers a range of financial products including Green Checking accounts (up to 4.50% APY), Certificates of Deposit (up to 4.10% APY), auto loans (as low as 4.40% APR), home loans, and online banking services. The credit union provides access to shared branches and ATM networks, allowing members to conduct transactions beyond their primary location. They also offer financial wellness resources through MoneyEdu and provide fraud protection education to members.

What distinguishes GECU is its emphasis on community engagement and member experience, including Youth Month programming and fraud awareness initiatives. The website highlights member testimonials praising the personal "family" feel of the institution. They offer competitive deposit rates and loan rates within the credit union space, along with digital banking convenience and access to shared branch networks.

As a credit union, GECU's member-owned structure typically results in better rates and lower fees than traditional banks, though membership eligibility may be limited to specific employee groups or communities. The institution appears stable and focused on member service, though the website provides limited detail about membership requirements, fee structures, or comprehensive product comparisons. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Green Checking accounts with interest earnings
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting as low as 4.40% APR
High-yield deposit accounts with Green Checking up to 4.50% APY and CDs up to 4.10% APY
Member-owned credit union structure typically results in lower fees and better rates than traditional banks
Access to shared branch network and ATM locator for convenient account access
Financial wellness resources through MoneyEdu program
Fraud protection and proactive fraud education for members
Youth financial literacy programs and community engagement initiatives
cons: Website does not clearly specify membership eligibility requirements or restrictions
Limited transparency on fee structure (checking account fees, overdraft fees, etc.) not disclosed on website
No mention of mobile app or mobile banking capabilities on the website
Single physical location in Rock Island, IL may limit accessibility for non-local members
Minimal product detail provided on website (e.g., home loan types, terms, credit requirements)
best_for: Employees of gas and electric utilities or eligible community members seeking competitive rates and personalized service
Auto buyers looking for low APR financing from a community-focused lender
Members prioritizing high-yield savings and CD accounts with a trustworthy, member-owned institution
diagnosis: Gas & Electric Credit Union is best for individuals eligible for membership who value personalized, community-focused banking with competitive rates on deposits and loans. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility may be restricted to specific employee groups or geographic areas, and the website lacks transparency about fees, membership requirements, and complete product details—prospective members should contact the credit union directly to confirm eligibility and full terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
